California 
Fair Political 
Practices Commission 
Walter White 
11124 Excelsior Dr., #21 
Norwalk, CA 
90650 
Dear Mr. White: 
September 3, 1987 
Re: 
Your Request for Advice 
Our File No. I-87-140 
Enclosed is a copy of the letter I mailed to you in June in 
response to your May letter. 
I cannot explain why you did not 
receive it. 
We have no record of your July 1 letter, nor do we have any 
check from you. 
As I explained in my June letter, campaign 
reports are not filed with us. 
You should contact the 
Secretary of State's office to obtain copies of these reports. 
DMG:plh 
Enclosure 
Sincerely, 
Diane M. Griffiths 
General Counsel 
428 J Street, Suite 800 • 
P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804~0807 • (916 L122~C;hh()

California 
Fair Political 
\.:; 
~ Practices Commission 
Walter E. White 
11124 Excelsior Dr., #21 
Norwalk, CA 
90650 
Dear Mr. White: 
June 15, 1987 
Re: 
Your Request for Advice 
Our File No. 87-140 
You have written seeking advice concerning the reporting 
obligations of the Confederation of Associations for the Unity 
of Societies of the Americas (CAUSA). 
Under the Political 
Reform Act (the "Act"),Y the Commission provides advice to 
persons whose duties under the Act are in question. 
(Section 
83114.) 
We do not provide advice to third parties about 
another persons's duties under the Act. 
(Regulation 
18329 (b) (8) (B) .) 
We do provide copies of documents filed with us to members 
of the public. 
However, campaign statements are not filed with 
the Commission. 
(See section 84215.) 
Sincerely, 
. 
. 
. 
0~tU'~fr5 
Diane M. Griffiths 
~ W-.

All references to 
regulations are to Title 2, Division 6 of the California 
Administrative Code.

TO: 
Fair Political Practices Commission 
P. O. Box 807 
Sacramento, California 
RE: 
CAUSA 
1. 
Enclosed is a copy of a Los Angeles Times article 
dated January 12, 1984 stated that CAUSA donated $5,000. 
to the Republican Party. 
Question: Was CAUSA required to file with the FPPC or 
the Registrar-Recorders office in Los Angeles to report 
the $5,000 donation? 
If so, was such a report filed with the FPPC. 
If such a report was filed, please advise cost of a copy 
of the filing. 
2. 
CAUSA in 1984 also paid for air fare and hotel bills 
for city councilmen and their wives for a trip to 
Washington D.C. 
The amount of money expended 
by CAUSA exceeded $l,OOO.The councilmen did not reportthe gifts. 
Question: 
Was CAUSA required to file with the FPPC for 
the gifts of air fare and hotel bills. 
CAUSA is headquartered in Washington D.C. 
CAUSA is a 
part of Rev. Moon's Unification Church which has branches 
in California. 
l» aJj-~ ~ l£4:~~~. 
Walter E. White 
11124 Excelsior Dr., 
#21 
Norwalk, 
Cal ifornia 90650

Los Angeles Republican Party 
leaders, incensed to learn that a 
recent statewide GOP youth confer-
ence was financed in part by an 
organization affiliated with the Rev. 
Sun Myung Moon's Unification 
Church of America, Wednesday 
fired the party worker who accept-
ed the group's donation. 
Jackie Clair, a fund raiser for the 
party, said Los Angeles GOP leaders 
demanded her resignation in the 
aftermath of a report by The Times 
~hat detailed how Clair had solicited 
I $5,000 contribution from the Con-
'ederation of Assns. for the Unity of 
he Societies of the Americas 
CA USA) to help finance the GOP 
'outh conference. The organization, 
n anti -communist group, receives 
)\l<:h of its funding from the Unifi-
ition Church. 
Called Inespoaa.lJ.e 
"They told me I've blackened the 
epublican Party's reputation all 
:f0S8 the state," said Clair, who 
w is treasurer of the California 
~publican Youth Caucus, the 
oup that sponsored the Dec. 29-
n. I conference at Lake Arrow-
ad "rm being cillled the 'Water-
teof Iss..: " 
Judy Ridgway, executive director 
.he Los Angeles County Republi-
I Party, termed Clair's decision to 
~t the $5,000 donation from the 
Il'Ch-related group a "totally ii-
xmsible act that showed very 
judgment on her part." Ridg-
, said the decision to fire Clair 
made by the party's seven-
rtber board of directors.
